purpose: Candidate research records for GS LinkOps AI database enrichment. Records require source-level validation before operational use.

records.name: SCORE free-placement optimization for LEO ground station networks
records.source_title: Free-Placement Optimization of Ground Station Locations for Low-Earth Orbit Satellites
records.summary: Introduces SCORE, a two-stage continuous-location optimization method for selecting or expanding ground station sites rather than choosing only from an existing provider catalogue. Evaluations use Capella Space, ICEYE and a synthetic Walker-Star constellation. The study reports up to 13% higher throughput than differential-evolution baselines, up to 15% higher total downlink than fixed-site methods, and retention of more than 92% of the gain when sites are constrained to proximity of existing fiber and power infrastructure.
records.confidence_level: High for the optimization method and reported experimental results; medium for direct commercial applicability.
pending_confirmation: Full objective function and weighting parameters
Terrain, horizon mask and radio-frequency interference treatment
Country permitting and spectrum authorization constraints
Fiber capacity, power quality and site acquisition costs
Applicability to customer-specific orbit and data-generation profiles
records.next_action: Add site-planning entities separate from operational stations, and support comparison of existing-site expansion versus greenfield deployment.

records.name: TeraNet three-node optical ground station network
records.source_title: Commissioning of the TeraNet Optical Ground Station Network
records.summary: Describes a three-node optical ground station network in Western Australia designed for missions from LEO to lunar distance. TN-1 uses a 70 cm aperture and ultra-sensitive detectors for lunar-distance links; TN-2 uses a 70 cm aperture with adaptive optics and single-mode-fiber coupling for coherent communications and precision timing; TN-3 is a 43 cm mobile optical station designed for rapid tactical deployment, quantum communications and quantum-assured time transfer. The paper reports remote network operations, on-orbit links and interoperability tests with other optical stations.
records.confidence_level: High for the published node architecture and commissioning claims; medium for current service availability and commercial access.
pending_confirmation: Exact optical wavelengths and modulation formats
Cloud statistics and annual availability by node
Maximum sustained throughput and link margin
Commercial access, pricing and service-level terms
records.next_action: Create optical_station, optical_terminal_capability and mobile_station_deployment tables, distinct from RF antenna assets.

records.name: Ground-based cyber threat taxonomy across LEO, MEO and GEO
records.source_title: Satellite Cybersecurity Across Orbital Altitudes: Analyzing Ground-Based Threats to LEO, MEO, and GEO
records.summary: Synthesizes publicly documented satellite-security incidents and compares ground-originated cyber-physical threats across LEO, MEO and GEO. It highlights RF-link exposure, weak encryption, command-path anomalies, software-update pathways and supply-chain weaknesses, and argues that threat profiles differ by orbital regime. For GS LinkOps AI, the main value is a structured risk taxonomy for station onboarding, mission-interface approval and supplier assessment rather than direct operational threat scoring.
database_targets: Supplier and Software Supply Chain Risk
records.confidence_level: Medium-high for taxonomy design and comparative risk framing; medium for individual incident completeness and quantitative prediction.
pending_confirmation: Underlying incident list and source quality per case
Separation of cyberattack, interference and equipment fault
Minimum encryption and authentication control baseline
Data-retention and disclosure policy for sensitive findings
records.next_action: Add security control evidence and incident provenance fields to provider, station, interface and mission-profile onboarding workflows.
